MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Hanowsky for and with Andrea Leung.

The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Pay Additional Extraordinary Atorney's Fees and Additional Extraordinary Administrator's Commission (subject to review of the order).

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-20-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robin Rudderow and Elizabeth Hanowsky for and with Andrea Leung. Thomas Shelton in Propria Persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Motion For Substitution of Attorney and the Petition for Instructions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court granted the Motion For Substition Of Attorney and denied the Petition For Instructions, (orders for the same were signed in open court). Mr. Shelton is to turn the files over to Ms. Rudderow within two weeks.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli/J. Gomez.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Substitution Of Attorney
Off Calendar May-20-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Petition For Letters Of Special Administration
Off Calendar Apr-23-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Shelton for and with Pyron Stewart (Petitioner). Daniel Conrad for and with Andrea Leung (Objector/Administrator). Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company.

The parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court denied the Ex Parte Petition For Letters Of Special Administration. Mr. Conrad will submit the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR#8052. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-21-2009 Continued To May-20-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robin Rudderow and Elizabeth Hanowsky for and with Andrea Leung (Administrator). Thomas Shelton in Propria Persona.

Mr. Shelton requested time to serve proper notice of the amended petition. Ms. Rudderow stated that Mr. Shelton did not sign the Substitution Of Attorney and that Ms. Leung would be filing a motion to relieve Mr. Shelton as Counsel. The matter was continued to May 20, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proper notice to Inheritance Funding, waiver of notice and the motion to substitute counsel.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Ex Parte Motion To Shorten Time And Advance Hearing Date
Off Calendar Apr-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Allocation On Account Of Statutory And Extraordinary Fees To Attorney
Off Calendar Jan-07-2009 Advanced From Mar-04-09 To Jan-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-13-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company (Interested Party). Thomas Shelton for and with Andrea Leung for the Special Administrator. James McElroy for and with Karen Ferguson.

Mr. Schroeder stated that Ms. Leung was ordered to marshall the assets, that he wanted to know if that had been accomplished, and if so, he did not oppose. Mr. Shelton stated that $287,185.04 had been moved to the estate account and noted that there was a home occupied by one party and other family members. Mr. Schroeder stated that he did not oppose based upon Ms. Leung being in place and the update by Mr. Shelton given today. Mr. McElroy stated that he objected to his client being referred to as recalcitrant, that there was an agreement that his client would move, that she was to obtain an agent to assist with the move, and that she would get $30,000; however, they never got the agreement in writing. Mr. Shelton reported that there is an order on file to release $30,000 but they can't do the distribution with special letters; they need the general letters. The Court appointed Andrea Leung as Administrator (order signed), ordered Ms. Ferguson to get the $30,000 preliminary distribution, and that it expected Ms. Ferguson to move out within three weeks. Mr. McElroy requested six weeks. The Court ordered Ms. Ferguson to move out four weeks from the date of the receipt of the $30,000, ordered that if the check issued today then Ms. Ferguson is to be out on or before February 6, 2009, and that the check was to go to Mr. McElroy's office. Mr. Shelton is to prepare the order regarding the terms of the move out. Mr. Shelton stated that the home was valued at $575,000. The Court stated the bond was sufficient. Discussions were held regarding Mr. Shelton's fee request; counsel and his client are to confer and contact the Court. The matter was submitted.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

Petition For Allocation On Account Of Statutory And Extraordinary Fees To Attorney
Off Calendar Jan-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Petition To Shorten Time And Advance Hearing Date

Petition For First Preliminary Distribution And Allowance On Acount To Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-24-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Shelton for Reba Toler.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the petition and that it wanted to start with the notice issues; stating that Leggit-Cook was not noticed. Mr. Shelton stated that he noticed Gwen Stuart, that there were problems with the executor not taking actions to benefit the estate, and that Leggit-Cook was brought in to make the executor perform. The Court then stated that Reba Toler was deceased and she was the child of Willa Toler. Mr. Shelton stated that Reba Toler had one son, that Mr. McElroy will not open the estate for two weeks, that he will represent Regis, and that Reba did not have a will. Discussions regarding the distribution of $30,000 to the four children were held. Mr. Shelton stated that there was a Unlawful Detainer (CUD-08-626499) against Karen, that she was to take the preliminary distribution and move, that he just found out that the case may involve more litigation, that he may have to refile the Unlawful Detainer, and that Karen Toler, Regis Schultz, Chablis Stewart, and her two children all reside in the residence. Mr. Shelton stated, that as to the attorney's fees, if he needs to do the ejectment and clear the house he will need funds. The Court denied the request for attorney's fees (stating that it will be counsel's responsibility to fund the litigation), and granted the release of the $30,000.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Linda Forster, CSR#13286. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-12-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Shelton for and with Bertha Joung (Proposed New Administrator). Reijess Shoats (Son of Reba Toler) in propria persona.

Mr. Shelton stated that he had not heard from Ms. Toler, represented to the Court that he spoke with Mr. Schroeder from Inheritance Funding, and stated that Mr. Schroeder had no objection to Ms. Joung being appointed. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). The bond was set at $856,000.00.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Shelton for Pyron Stewart. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Shelton stated that Bertha Juong (Professional Fiduciary) accepted to serve as Administrator, that Inheritance Funding had approved and waived notice and that all Parties had agreed to place Gwendolyn's share into a blocked account. Mr. shelton also stated that he filed the amendment to the petition on October 10, 2008. The Court directed Mr. Shelton to file the Amended Petition so that a hearing date could be set and took today's matter off calendar. The Court denied the request to shorten time.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Mann.

The matter was continued to October 14, 2008 for Mr. Schroeder to present argument to support his position.
